sound_type: Ten Pacific Wrens singing simultaneously in wet old-growth during a storm -- maximum territorial chorus
era: Storm (Pacific Northwest winter/spring storm season). Year-round resident but storm-chorus behavior documented in winter/spring. Annual occurrence in optimal old-growth habitat.
ecosystem: Wet old-growth forest (western hemlock / Sitka spruce / western red cedar old-growth, Olympic Peninsula, western Cascades, Coast Range Oregon)
season: Storm (winter/spring storm season; countersinging behavior most documented in winter storms)

theory_nodes.concept: Pacific Wren biology: Troglodytes pacificus, 9-11 grams body mass, elevated to full species 2010 from Winter Wren subspecies. Old-growth and mature forest understory specialist: western hemlock, Sitka spruce, western red cedar; fern mats, nurse logs, root tangles, stream-edge vegetation. Year-round resident. Song: 5-10 seconds, ~36 notes/trills in rapid succession, 80-100 dB at 1 meter. Highest volume-to-body-mass ratio of any Pacific Northwest bird. Function: territorial defense and mate attraction. Storm behavior: countersinging (singing more intensively during storms/high wind to maintain territorial presence under acoustically compromised conditions).
theory_nodes.description: Pacific Wren -- 10 grams, highest vol:mass ratio PNW birds, countersinging storm behavior.

theory_nodes.concept: Ten-wren maximum configuration: in optimal Pacific Northwest old-growth habitat, territory density 2-4 per hectare (possibly higher in densest Olympic Peninsula stands). Ten singing males = approximately 3-5 hectares of optimal old-growth acoustically saturated. Sequential ignition from single stimulus (storm, predator alarm) propagates through ten adjacent territories within seconds. Result: all ten males singing simultaneously, every point in the stand within hearing distance of 1-3 birds simultaneously, entire acoustic environment occupied. Total mass: 100 grams. Stand filled completely.
theory_nodes.description: Ten-wren simultaneous configuration -- 3-5 ha stand fully occupied, sequential ignition, 100 grams total.

theory_nodes.concept: Old-growth acoustic properties: multi-layered canopy creates high-reverberation environment (sounds persist longer, travel farther within forest, layer over reflected signals). Nurse log/root wad matrix causes acoustic diffraction -- wren song reaches listeners from multiple directions simultaneously. Storm amplification: wet vegetation increases reflectivity; rain white noise (lower-frequency dominant) creates backdrop against which wren's high-frequency complex song stands out with greater spectral definition. Wren song evolved for reverberant old-growth acoustic environment.
theory_nodes.description: Old-growth acoustic properties -- reverberation, diffraction, storm amplification.

theory_nodes.concept: The most intimate E=10 calibration: ten birds, 100 grams total, one stand (3-5 ha). Smallest-scale E=10 in the engine. E=10 calibrated by output-to-capacity ratio, not absolute scale. A 10-gram bird at maximum output fills its acoustic environment as completely as an orca fills the Salish Sea. The closing sequence arc: exterior detonation (354, coastal cliff) → foundational emergence (355, major river) → intimate interior maximum (356, one stand) → matriarchal coordination (357) → full biodiversity (358) → silence (359). Intimacy is deepening not reduction.
theory_nodes.description: Most intimate E=10 -- output-to-capacity ratio; intimacy is deepening not reduction.

theory_nodes.concept: Pairing with The Fartz: both fill their environment completely regardless of scale. The Fartz: Capitol Hill clubs, small audiences, Alternative Tentacles thousands of records -- maximum output per unit, the room filled. Pacific Wren: 10 grams, one stand -- maximum output per unit, the stand filled. E=10 where capacity fully realized. Same calibration principle in two different contexts.
theory_nodes.description: Pairing rationale: same E=10 principle -- fill the capacity completely, scale irrelevant.

cross_references.relationship: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Same calibration: maximum output from small source, environment filled completely. The Fartz: Capitol Hill room. Pacific Wren: old-growth stand. Scale different; principle identical.
Scale contraction: millions of stoneflies (major river) → ten wrens (3-5 ha stand). The closing sequence moves from maximum number to maximum density. Intimacy increases.
Next entry. Shift from intimate old-growth interior (one stand, one species) to open Salish Sea (matriarchal coordination, apex predator). From smallest E=10 to strategic maximum.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. The intimate E=10. Both: maximum output from a small source, the environment filled completely. Pacific Wren (100 grams total, one stand, storm) + The Fartz (Capitol Hill, small clubs, Alternative Tentacles). The loudest sound from the smallest source. E=10 is the ratio of output to capacity.

milestones: THE MOST INTIMATE E=10 IN THE ENGINE
